basic_details.job_title: ISRO SDSC SHAR Teacher Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 10 Trained Graduate Teacher, Primary Teacher Posts
basic_details.short_information: SDSC SHAR, Sriharikota, under the Department of Space, ISRO, has invited online applications for 10 Teacher posts (Advertisement No. SDSC SHAR/RMT/01/2026 dated 16.06.2026) to staff its Space Central Schools at Sriharikota and Sullurpeta. Online registration opened on 16 June 2026 and closes on 07 July 2026 (11:59 PM), with selection through a Written Test and Skill Test.

application_fee.details: Here's what candidates need to pay and when they can expect a refund, since SDSC SHAR follows a two-part fee structure unlike a flat one-time fee. | All candidates pay Rs. 750 online at the time of application submission, since no other payment mode is accepted. Refunds are processed only for candidates who appear in the Written Test: women, SC, ST, PwBD, and Ex-Servicemen candidates (who are exempt from the application fee) get the full Rs. 750 back, while all other candidates get Rs. 500 back after the application fee is deducted. SDSC SHAR is not responsible for pending or failed transactions, so candidates are advised to register and pay well before the closing date.

age_limit.details: This section answers how much these teaching posts pay and what the upper age cut-off is, since both vary by post code under the 7th CPC pay matrix. | Age relaxation is available as follows: 5 years for SC/ST candidates, 3 years for OBC candidates (against posts reserved for these categories), 10 years for women of all categories, and 10 years for PwBD candidates (15 years for ST, 13 years for OBC, 10 years for General/EWS). Ex-Servicemen and Central Government employees get relaxation as per Government of India orders. All concessions are concurrent, not cumulative - only the highest single permissible relaxation applies, and PwBD women do not get an additional age concession under the women's category. | In-Hand Salary Calculator 2026

qualification.details: Each post carries a distinct qualification requirement linked to the subject taught. The table-style breakdown below summarises the core academic and teaching-eligibility requirements for every post code. | Candidates need a four-year NCTE-recognised integrated degree with at least 50% marks (subject + B.Ed component), OR a Bachelor's/Honours degree with 50% marks plus a B.Ed degree from an NCTE-recognised institution (alternatives include a 3-year integrated B.Ed-M.Ed or a 1-year B.Ed Special Education from RCI). Candidates must also have qualified CTET Paper-II and be able to teach in Hindi and English. Applicants must have studied Mathematics as a main subject for at least 3 years/6 semesters, Physics for at least 1 year/2 semesters, and one of Chemistry/Electronics/Computer Science/Statistics for at least 1 year/2 semesters. Candidates with B.Sc (Hons.) in Physics or Chemistry are not eligible for this post. | The qualification pattern mirrors TGT (Maths): a four-year integrated degree or a Bachelor's/Honours degree (50% marks) with Chemistry, plus a B.Ed degree, along with a qualifying CTET Paper-II score. Candidates must have studied Biology/Life Science/Botany/Zoology as a Honours or main subject for at least 3 years/6 semesters, combined with Chemistry, Botany, or Zoology as applicable for at least one year or two semesters. | This post requires a Bachelor's degree in Electrical Engineering, Electronics Engineering, or Electronics & Communication Engineering with at least 50% marks, along with a B.Ed degree (50% marks) from an NCTE-recognised institution, ability to teach in Hindi and English, and knowledge of computers. One year of practical workshop/factory experience is desirable but not mandatory. | Candidates need a Bachelor's degree with Telugu as the main subject (or one of three equal optional subjects), a BOL in Telugu, or a Postgraduate degree in Telugu, combined with a B.Ed with Telugu methodology or Telugu Pandit Training. Candidates must have passed State TET or CTET Paper-II with Telugu as one of the chosen subjects. | Eligible candidates need Senior Secondary/Intermediate (10+2) with 50% marks plus a 2-year Diploma in Elementary Education, OR Class XII with 50% marks plus a 4-year B.El.Ed, OR Class XII with 50% marks plus a 2-year RCI Diploma in Special Education. A qualifying score in CTET Paper-I and the ability to teach in Hindi and English are also required. | Across all posts, Hindi working knowledge is necessary, and candidates claiming equivalent qualifications must produce documentary evidence at the time of the Skill Test. | DON'T MISSRRB 6565 Technician Online Form 2026 (Short Notice)6,565 Posts • Technician • RRB29 Jul 20266,565 postsOSSSC 5989 Nursing Officer Online Form 20265,989 Vacancies • Nursing Officer13 Jul 20265,989 postsGSRTC 4599 Driver Online Form 20264,599 Openings • GSRTC05 Jul 20264,599 postsGSRTC 4318 Conductor Online Form 20264,318 Posts • GSRTC05 Jul 20264,318 postsKSP Civil Police Constable Online Form 20263,991 Vacancies • Civil Police Constable • KSP03 Jul 20263,991 postsMPSC 2619 Group C Online Form 20262,619 Openings • Group C17 Jul 20262,619 posts

vacancy_details.Post Name: Trained Graduate Teacher (Maths) - to teach Maths & Physics
Trained Graduate Teacher (Science) - to teach Chemistry & Biology
Trained Graduate Teacher - Work Experience Teacher (WET)

selection_process: Post Subject-Wise Question Split Minimum Qualifying Marks
TGT (Maths) 50% Maths + 50% Physics 40% each subject; 50% aggregate
TGT (Science) 40% Biology + 30% Chemistry + 30% Physics 40% each subject; 50% aggregate
SDSC SHAR shortlists and selects teachers through a two-stage process - a written exam followed by a practical skill demonstration - explained below with the exact marking scheme.
The Computer Based Test checks both the theoretical and practical knowledge of candidates across the prescribed syllabus. For Primary Teacher
TGT (Maths)
TGT (Science)
and TGT (WET)
the paper carries 180 marks split into four parts - Languages (20)
General Awareness/Reasoning/Computers (20)
Education & Leadership Perspective (40)
and Subject Specific (100). For TGT (Telugu)
the 180-mark paper covers General English/Awareness/Reasoning (20)
Education Perspectives (20)
Pedagogy (40)
and Telugu Language & Literature (100).
Based on Written Test performance
candidates are shortlisted for the Skill Test
generally in a 1:5 ratio with a minimum of 10 candidates category-wise.
The Skill Test is purely qualifying in nature
evaluated out of 100 marks

with a minimum qualifying score of 60%. It is a chalk-and-board based test where each candidate is given a subject-related topic and must demonstrate subject knowledge and teaching ability by conducting a mock class.
Final selection is based purely on Written Test marks among candidates who clear the Skill Test
with empanelment done as per the number of notified vacancies. In case of a tie
the academic score of the essential qualification (PG/Graduation/Senior Secondary
as applicable) is the first tie-breaker
followed by the candidate's age (senior candidates get preference) as the second tie-breaker.
how_to_apply: SDSC SHAR accepts applications exclusively through its online portal. The steps below summarise the process candidates need to follow.
Visit the official SDSC SHAR website: www.shar.gov.in.
Locate the online registration link for Advertisement No. SDSC SHAR/RMT/01/2026.
Register and fill in the application form for the desired post code.
Upload the photograph, signature, and required certificates in the specified format and size.
Pay the Rs. 750 fee online (no other payment mode is accepted).
Submit the application and download the registration confirmation form for future reference.
A separate application and separate fee payment is mandatory for each post; candidates cannot submit multiple applications for the same post, and incomplete applications will not be entertained.
Also Read: Haryana HSSC CET 2026 Group D Notification Out - Apply Online

faq.question: Q1. How many teacher vacancies has SDSC SHAR announced for 2026?
faq.answer: SDSC SHAR has announced 10 vacancies across five post codes: TGT Maths, TGT Science, TGT WET, TGT Telugu, and Primary Teacher.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for SDSC SHAR Teacher Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online registration window closes on 07 July 2026 at 23:59 Hrs.

faq.question: Q3. What is the application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: Candidates must pay Rs. 750 (Rs. 250 application fee + Rs. 500 processing fee) at the time of submission.

faq.question: Q4. Is the application fee refundable?
faq.answer: Yes, but only for candidates who appear in the Written Test. Women, SC, ST, PwBD, and Ex-Servicemen get a full Rs. 750 refund; other candidates get Rs. 500 back.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for SDSC SHAR Teacher posts?
faq.answer: TGT posts (Code 01-04) require candidates to be 18-35 years, and the Primary Teacher post (Code 05) requires 18-30 years, both as on 07 July 2026.

faq.question: Q6. What is the pay level for these teaching posts?
faq.answer: TGT posts carry Pay Matrix Level 7 (Rs. 44,900 - Rs. 1,42,400), while the Primary Teacher post carries Level 6 (Rs. 35,400 - Rs. 1,12,400).

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for SDSC SHAR Teacher Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Selection involves a Written Test (CBT) followed by a qualifying Skill Test; final merit is based purely on Written Test marks among Skill Test qualifiers.

faq.question: Q8. Is CTET mandatory for these posts?
faq.answer: Yes, TGT posts require a qualifying score in CTET Paper-II (or State TET Paper-II for TGT Telugu), and the Primary Teacher post requires CTET Paper-I.

faq.question: Q9. Can a candidate apply for more than one post in this recruitment?
faq.answer: Yes, but a separate application and separate fee payment is required for each post; a candidate cannot submit multiple applications for the same post.

faq.question: Q10. Where will the Written Test be conducted?
faq.answer: The Computer Based Test will be held online across 22 cities in India, including Chennai, Hyderabad, Bangalore, New Delhi, Mumbai, and Kolkata, subject to candidate availability per city.
